title: Check For Missing Dimension Or Weight Data When Chutes Appear To Overfill
summary: Use this source-backed diagnostic to determine whether apparent chute overfilling is explained by missing or incomplete dimension or weight data on the affected sort induct path. The source states tote fullness and chute close behavior are based on weight and dimensions in software, not local chute sensors or photo eyes.
when_to_use: Use when chutes appear to be overfilling and you need to verify whether the behavior is consistent with missing or incomplete dimension or weight data rather than a chute-sensor issue.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to diagnose local chute sensor or photo-eye faults for tote fullness behavior, because the source states there are no chute sensors or photo eyes for this function.
Do not use this runbook as a recovery procedure; the source supports diagnosis and escalation only, not corrective recovery steps.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Assess the reported overfill condition, verify the source-backed system behavior, check whether dimension and weight data are being received, document any missing or incomplete data, and escalate when indicated.

safety_notes: This source describes a diagnostic review only and does not provide physical intervention steps.
Do not assume a local chute sensor fault from this source, because the source states tote fullness behavior is not driven by chute sensors or photo eyes.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to system data or views showing received dimension and weight data
Source-backed understanding of tote fullness dependency on dimension and weight data

access_required: Access to system data or views showing received dimension and weight data
system_preconditions: An affected sort induct path or chute overfill symptom has been identified for review.
external_references: UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1 source segment covering tote fullness and missing dimension data behavior.

visual_references.description: Training slide describing parcel lifecycle into a tote, software-based tote fullness calculation using dimension and weight data, and chute closure behavior when a tote is full.

steps.title: Identify the apparent overfill condition
steps.purpose: Confirm the symptom being investigated matches the source-supported diagnostic scenario.
steps.instruction: Identify the condition where chutes appear to be overfilling on the affected area or sort induct path.
steps.expected_result: A specific apparent chute overfill condition is identified for diagnostic review.
steps.healthy_condition: The reported symptom is clearly defined and tied to an affected chute or path.
steps.failure_condition: The symptom cannot be clearly identified or is too vague to evaluate against the source guidance.

actions.label: Confirm overfill symptom
actions.exact_action: Identify the condition where chutes appear to be overfilling.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Review the slide context showing tote fullness calculation and chute unavailable behavior when a tote is considered full.

stop_or_escalate_if: The overfill symptom cannot be tied to a specific affected path or chute for review.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If dimension data is missing, all the chutes can appear to overfill.

steps.title: Confirm fullness logic depends on weight and dimensions
steps.purpose: Anchor the diagnosis to the source-backed system behavior before checking for data gaps.
steps.instruction: Verify from the source-backed system understanding that chute fullness decisions rely on dimension and weight data rather than photo eyes or other chute sensors.
steps.expected_result: You confirm that the source attributes tote fullness and chute close behavior to software using weight and dimensions, not local chute sensors.
steps.healthy_condition: The diagnostic is based on the source statement that there are no chute sensors or photo eyes for this function.
steps.failure_condition: The issue is being treated as a chute-sensor problem without source support.

actions.label: Review source-backed dependency
actions.exact_action: Verify from the source-backed system understanding that chute fullness decisions rely on dimension and weight data rather than photo eyes or other chute sensors.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the slide and transcript context describing software tote fullness calculation using weight and dimensions and the absence of chute sensors/photo eyes.

stop_or_escalate_if: Others are pursuing a local chute sensor or photo-eye fault theory based on this source alone.

source_evidence.summary: The source explains that tote fullness and chute close decisions rely on dimension and weight data rather than local chute sensors or photo eyes.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: We don't have any photo eyes; there's no sensors at all, just weight and dimensions. If dimension data is missing, all the chutes can appear to overfill.

steps.title: Check whether dimension and weight data are being received
steps.purpose: Determine whether the affected path has the data inputs the source says are required for fullness behavior.
steps.instruction: Check whether dimension data and weight data are being received for the affected sort induct path.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether both dimension and weight data are present for the affected path.
steps.healthy_condition: Dimension data and weight data appear present for the affected sort induct path.
steps.failure_condition: Dimension data or weight data is missing, absent, or not being received for the affected path.

actions.label: Review received data
actions.exact_action: Check whether dimension data and weight data are being received for the affected sort induct path.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Review the parcel lifecycle context showing upstream messages and dimensional data before and at sorter induction.
Review the induction messaging context showing dimensions provided in upstream messages and dimensional data on the sorter.
Review the tote fullness context showing software updates tote cube/fullness using dimension and weight data from sort induct.

stop_or_escalate_if: Dimension data is missing for the affected path.
Weight data is missing for the affected path.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: A first message from Chat provides tracking ID, dimensions, and package type. A second message from Chat provides tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.

If this message fails and an unknown parcel is inducted, OptiSweep assigns default dimensions. It then describes a second Chat message containing tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data on the sorter, and potential destination data.
Software adds the parcel to the tote location in the rack and updates tote cube or fullness using dimension and weight data from sort induct.

steps.title: Identify specific missing or incomplete data elements
steps.purpose: Capture the exact data gap that may explain the apparent overfill behavior.
steps.instruction: If data is incomplete, note the specific missing elements described by the source example, such as missing weight or incomplete dimension values.
steps.expected_result: The missing or incomplete data elements are documented clearly enough for escalation.
steps.healthy_condition: No missing or incomplete dimension or weight elements are observed.
steps.failure_condition: Weight data is missing, dimension data is missing, or dimension values are incomplete for the affected path.

actions.label: Document data gaps
actions.exact_action: If data is incomplete, note the specific missing elements described by the source example, such as missing weight or incomplete dimension values.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the slide context on fullness calculation and nearby transcript discussing issues caused by missing dimension or weight data.

stop_or_escalate_if: Any required dimension data is missing.
Dimension values appear incomplete for the affected path.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If dimension data is missing, all the chutes can appear to overfill.
Nearby transcript also discusses priority behavior around approximately 60 percent, 70 percent, and 80 percent fullness, chute closure, and issues caused by missing dimension or weight data.

steps.title: Record findings and escalate if indicated
steps.purpose: Preserve the source-backed conclusion and hand off unresolved issues appropriately.
steps.instruction: Record that missing dimension or weight data may explain the overfill behavior and preserve the observed data gap for escalation.
steps.expected_result: A source-backed diagnostic finding is documented, including whether missing or incomplete data plausibly explains the symptom.
steps.healthy_condition: Findings are documented and either support a data-gap explanation or show that data appears present.
steps.failure_condition: The issue remains unresolved without documented findings, or overfill persists despite data appearing present.

actions.label: Document and escalate
actions.exact_action: Record that missing dimension or weight data may explain the overfill behavior and preserve the observed data gap for escalation.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference the source slide that ties tote fullness and chute closure behavior to weight and dimensions.

stop_or_escalate_if: Dimension or weight data is missing or incomplete for the affected path.
Overfill behavior persists even though dimension and weight data appear present, because the source does not provide further recovery steps.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: We don't have any photo eyes; there's no sensors at all, just weight and dimensions. If dimension data is missing, all the chutes can appear to overfill.

source_evidence.candidate_note: Escalate if dimension or weight data is missing or incomplete for the affected path. Escalate if overfill behavior persists even though dimension and weight data appear present, because the source does not provide further recovery steps.

success_criteria: You determine whether missing or incomplete dimension or weight data is a plausible source-backed explanation for the apparent chute overfill behavior.
Any observed data gap is documented for escalation.
healthy_conditions: Dimension and weight data are being received for the affected sort induct path.
The diagnostic conclusion is documented using the source-backed understanding that fullness behavior depends on weight and dimensions.
failure_conditions: Dimension data is missing for the affected path.
Weight data is missing for the affected path.
Dimension values are incomplete for the affected path.
Overfill behavior persists even though dimension and weight data appear present.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if dimension or weight data is missing or incomplete for the affected path.
Escalate if overfill behavior persists even though dimension and weight data appear present, because the source does not provide further recovery steps.
Do not escalate this source finding as a chute-sensor or photo-eye fault based on this source alone, because the source states there are no chute sensors or photo eyes for this function.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training slide showing parcel-to-tote lifecycle, software fullness calculation using dimension and weight data, and chute unavailable behavior when full.
Training slide showing upstream parcel messages and dimensional data before and at sorter induction.
Training slide showing induction messaging and dimensional data on the sorter.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: We don't have any photo eyes; there's no sensors at all, just weight and dimensions. If dimension data is missing, all the chutes can appear to overfill.

missing_details: The source does not specify the exact system screen, query, or interface to use when checking received dimension and weight data.
The source does not provide exact field names, acceptable value formats, or thresholds for determining incomplete data beyond the general examples of missing dimension or weight data.
The source does not provide corrective recovery steps after identifying the data gap.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this diagnostic.
